summary.narrative: Governor Polis entered office in 2019 with focus on education, healthcare, climate, housing, and public safety. Early term achievements include healthcare expansion (abortion/gender-affirming care access), education funding increases ($500M+ in 2024), climate progress (87.5% toward 2030 GHG targets), and cost-of-living initiatives. Mixed record includes vetoing water pipeline for rural communities and blocking federal research facility, raising accountability questions.
summary.grade_rationale: Strong delivery on healthcare and education priorities with measurable outcomes; climate progress documented. Deductions for apparent contradictions (cost-of-living promises vs. vetoing rural infrastructure) and limited transparency on housing metrics. Clemency consideration for election denier suggests selective application of principles.

promises.normalized: Increase K-12 education funding and eliminate Budget Stabilization Factor
Enshrine access to abortion and gender-affirming healthcare in law
Reduce cost of living and improve housing affordability
Achieve climate goals with 2030 GHG emissions reduction targets

data_gaps: Specific measurable outcomes for housing affordability initiatives
Public safety improvement metrics and program details
Teacher loan relief program implementation and participation data
Student mental health investment allocation and results
Water pipeline decision rationale and rural impact assessment
Federal atmospheric research center closure justification
Cost-of-living savings quantification by household type
Detailed election margin and approval rating trends
